pi_items.source: Task specification (task.json description lines 5-14). This is a synthetic internal-only URL used for testing the event rule configuration.
Task specification (task.json description lines 15-24). This is a synthetic internal-only URL used for testing the event rule configuration.
Task specification (task.json description line 18, metadata line 19). Specified as the name for the Soft Trigger rule.

Web search of Nx Witness documentation: The official documented macro for Generic Events is {event.source}, not {event.sourceName}. According to Network Optix support articles, {event.source} will be replaced by the content in the 'Source contains' field. {event.sourceName} does not appear in any official Nx Witness documentation (web search returned zero results for {event.sourceName} Nx Witness). The verifier code accepts both {event.sourceName} and {event.source} as correct (line 73 of verifier.py), but the task.json metadata claims {event.sourceName} which contradicts the official documentation.

Web search of Nx Witness documentation reveals critical issues: (1) {user.name} is not a documented Nx Witness macro variable. Web search returned zero results for {user.name} as a Nx Witness event rule macro. (2) More importantly, Soft Trigger events do NOT support any macro variables at all. According to Network Optix support articles (https://support.networkoptix.com/hc/en-us/community/posts/1500000832681), 'There are no variables (aka placeholders) available for the Soft Trigger event. Only for Generic Event and Analytics Event.' The supported placeholders for events that do support them are: {event.caption}, {event.description}, {event.cameraId}, {event.cameraName}, {event.eventType}, {event.eventName}, and {event.source}. Since Soft Triggers support zero variables, neither {user.name} nor {event.cameraId} can be used in Soft Trigger HTTP request actions.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures two Nx Witness VMS event rules: (1) Storage Failure rule sends POST requests to https://ops-api.internal/webhooks/health, and (2) Soft Trigger rule named 'Panic Alert' sends POST requests to https://ops-api.internal/webhooks/panic. The environment uses Nx Witness version 5.1.5 or 6.0.1, deployed locally at https://localhost:7001. However, the metadata claims contain inaccurate macro variable specifications: the Health rule metadata requires {event.sourceName} which should be {event.source}, and the Panic rule metadata requires {user.name} and {event.cameraId} but Soft Trigger events do not support any variable macros according to official Nx Witness documentation.
